sections.question: 1) May a registered lobbyist purchase tickets to a fundraising dinner hosted by the controlled committee of an elected state officer? 2) May a lobbyist's spouse purchase such tickets? 3) May a lobbyist attend such a dinner if someone else purchased the tickets?
sections.conclusion: A lobbyist may not purchase tickets to a fundraising dinner hosted by a controlled committee of an elected state officer, as ticket purchases constitute contributions prohibited by Sections 85200 and 86202. A lobbyist's spouse may purchase tickets if acting entirely independently and for purposes unrelated to the lobbyist's lobbying activities. A lobbyist may attend a fundraising dinner if someone else purchased the tickets, but may not make contributions, act as an agent/intermediary, or arrange contributions.
sections.question_synthetic: The document addresses three related questions about whether a registered lobbyist and their spouse may purchase or attend fundraising dinner tickets hosted by controlled committees of elected state officers.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Registered lobbyists are prohibited from purchasing fundraising dinner tickets as this constitutes a prohibited contribution. Their spouses may purchase tickets if acting independently, and lobbyists may attend dinners purchased by others, but cannot facilitate or arrange contributions in any capacity.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks traditional 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers but content clearly follows this structure. Minor OCR errors present ('Callfortya', 'hobbyist' instead of 'lobbyist') but do not impede comprehension. Letter dated April 29, 1977.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1) May a registered lobbyist purchase tickets to a fundraising dinner hosted by the controlled committee of an elected state officer? 2) May a lobbyist's spouse purchase such tickets? 3) May a lobbyist attend such a dinner if someone else purchased the tickets?

CONCLUSION: A lobbyist may not purchase tickets to a fundraising dinner hosted by a controlled committee of an elected state officer, as ticket purchases constitute contributions prohibited by Sections 85200 and 86202. A lobbyist's spouse may purchase tickets if acting entirely independently and for purposes unrelated to the lobbyist's lobbying activities. A lobbyist may attend a fundraising dinner if someone else purchased the tickets, but may not make contributions, act as an agent/intermediary, or arrange contributions.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to inquiries by a registered lobbyist regarding the legality of purchasing or attending fundraising dinner tickets hosted by controlled committees of elected state officers under the Political Reform Act.
